eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Liberal Democrat Peter Taylor commands 95% trader consensus as Watford mayoral frontrunner, driven by his unchallenged record since 2018—including re-election in 2022—and Watford Liberal Democrats' projected supermajority of 31 council seats under first-past-the-post voting. Nominations finalized April 9 confirmed a fragmented seven-candidate field, with Labour's Keith Morgan at 3%, Conservatives' Abdul Laskar at 1%, and others trailing, diluting opposition votes. Taylor's March 30 manifesto launch emphasized local priorities amid routine campaigning, with no public polls or disruptions shifting sentiment. The May 7 election looms; upset scenarios include a late scandal, abstentionist turnout drop, or unforeseen opposition consolidation, though historical precedents favor continuity.

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

events.title: F1 Drivers' Champion
events.description:

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' superior chassis and power unit under 2026's new active aerodynamics regulations have propelled George Russell and rookie Kimi Antonelli to the top of the drivers' standings after three Grands Prix, with Antonelli's pole-to-win at the Japanese GP—his second consecutive victory—extending his lead to nine points despite Russell's consistent podium finishes. Trader consensus reflects Russell's edge through greater experience managing intra-team pressure and race pace, amid an extended April break following Bahrain and Saudi cancellations that allowed tire testing and Miami upgrades, where rivals like Ferrari's Charles Leclerc and McLaren's Oscar Piastri eye convergence. Lower probabilities for Max Verstappen and others stem from Red Bull's early struggles, leaving the Mercedes duel as the championship focal point ahead of the Sprint weekend.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters around 21–23°C (78% combined implied probability) for Qingdao's highest temperature on May 2, reflecting the latest forecast model ensembles from ECMWF, GFS, and China Meteorological Administration (CMA) that project daytime highs in this narrow range amid a cool early-May pattern. Over the past 48 hours, persistent southerly winds and thickening cloud cover observed in Qingdao—evident in recent CMA monitoring and satellite imagery—have tempered earlier warmer outlooks, suppressing peaks via reduced solar insolation and enhanced sea breeze effects from the Yellow Sea. Differentiating factors include model disagreements on afternoon boundary layer mixing and cloud dissipation timing, potentially varying highs by 1–2°C; historical early-May averages hover near 20°C. New 00Z/12Z runs due today could sharpen resolution as the event nears.

markets.question: Will Tesla (TSLA) close above $350 on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on May 1 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla shares hovered around $374-$379 during April 30 trading, digesting Q1 2026 earnings reported April 22 that delivered adjusted EPS of $0.41—beating consensus by $0.02—alongside 16% revenue growth to $22.39 billion and auto gross margins of 21.1%, offset by elevated 2026 capital expenditures exceeding $25 billion for AI, Optimus robotics, and energy storage. Trader consensus, reflected in analyst average price targets near $400-$406 (ranging $220-$428), prices in accelerating Cybercab production at Giga Texas, unsupervised robotaxi rollout in Texas cities, and Optimus Gen 3 low-volume ramp targeted for summer. With the May 1 close imminent, technical resistance near $380 and daily market flows will be pivotal amid ongoing EV competition and macroeconomic risk appetite.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 500-519 tweets from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

The 200-219 outcome at 27.5% reflects potential for a final-day uptick, while lower priors like 160-179 trail due to no observed slowdowns; prior weeks varied 150-300, but this period's consistent volume—spanning SpaceX, Tesla, and policy replies—anchors mid-range expectations absent major catalysts.

markets.question: Will Z.ai have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Jeddah be 39°C or higher on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sa/jeddah/OEJN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Pakistan military action against Kabul by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Pakistan initiates a drone, missile, or air strike within the municipality of Kabul by the specified date Afghanistan Time (GMT+4:30). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Pakistani military forces that impact within one of the 22 municipal districts of Kabul.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land within the municipality of Kabul or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Pakistani ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

Despite China-mediated talks in early April yielding pledges to explore de-escalation and avoid escalation, cross-border mortar and rocket attacks persisted, with Pakistan blamed for strikes on April 27 killing four and wounding 70 in Afghanistan. markets.question: Counter-Strike: Banger Gang vs hindsight (BO1) - ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Banger Gang and hindsight in the ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 30 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Banger Gang" if Banger Gang win the match against hindsight.

This market will resolve to "hindsight" if hindsight win the match against Banger Gang.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Banger Gang vs hindsight (BO1) - ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Banger Gang and hindsight in the ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 30 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Banger Gang" if Banger Gang win the match against hindsight.

This market will resolve to "hindsight" if hindsight win the match against Banger Gang.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Banger Gang heads into this BO1 ESEA Advanced Europe Season 57 regular season matchup on a two-match win streak, defeating tincan 1-0 and FC Famalicão Esports 1-0 over the past two days, bolstering their momentum in the group stage. Hindsight, an all-female squad ranked #282 on HLTV with players like Ann4 and Madam averaging around 1.0 ratings, aims to rebound from a 0-1 loss to Clutchain Female yesterday and earlier ESEA defeats including 0-1 to LFO on April 16. The single-map format heightens volatility through map vetoes, pistol rounds, and economy swings, while Banger Gang's Turkish roster brings stronger recent form against hindsight's 20% win rate over the last three months. Standings pressure both in the competitive league pushing toward playoffs.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades around 1.168 as of April 30, 2026, reflecting persistent U.S. monetary policy advantage amid a 160 basis point Fed-ECB rate gap, with Fed funds at 3.5-3.75% versus ECB main refinancing operations at 2.15%. Eurozone CPI accelerated to 2.6% year-over-year in March, up from 1.9% in February, curbing ECB easing bets ahead of today's Governing Council decision and press conference. U.S. inflation also ticked higher, supporting trader consensus for limited Fed cuts. Forecasts imply EUR/USD ranging 1.14-1.22 through year-end, hinging on labor data and growth outlooks; watch June 16-17 FOMC and ECB meetings for divergence shifts that could breach key technical levels.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% in its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—the third straight hold—amid accelerating March CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year and steady unemployment at 4.3%, signaling persistent price pressures despite solid job gains. This stance aligns with the March dot plot's median projection for rates near 3% by end-2027, while fed funds futures price a flat path around 3.6% through early 2027, reflecting trader consensus for minimal easing. Polymarket sentiment aggregates this skin-in-the-game view, emphasizing economic resilience over cuts. Watch April CPI on May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC for potential shifts in rate path expectations.

markets.question: Spread: Clube do Remo (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 2 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Clube do Remo" if Clube do Remo win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Botafogo FR".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Botafogo FR vs. Clube do Remo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 2 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Botafogo FR hosts Clube do Remo in Brazilian Série A action on May 2 at Estádio Nilton Santos, where home advantage and superior standing fuel trader focus. Botafogo, mid-table at around 12 points after a gritty 2-1 away win over Vasco da Gama on April 5, boasts a 4-0-5 record amid inconsistent form but strong home splits. Remo languishes lower with 6 points from a 1-3-5 mark, reeling from a 0-2 loss to Santos on April 2 and poor away results (zero wins in recent outings). Key Remo absences like defender Diego Hernández (knee) and midfielder Eduardo Melo (thigh) through late April weaken their squad, while limited head-to-head history shows mixed results from 2021 Série B clashes. Schedule spots offer Botafogo rest before facing Coritiba and Chapecoense, heightening stakes in the tightly contested mid-table and relegation battles.

markets.question: Pam Bondi testifies before congress by May 31?

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: House Oversight Committee announced on April 29 that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will appear for a transcribed deposition on May 29 regarding the Justice Department's handling of Jeffrey Epstein files, solidifying trader consensus at 82.5% for her testifying before Congress by May 31. This follows a bipartisan subpoena issued in March, her failure to attend prior dates like April 14, and Democrats' recent civil contempt resolution—prompting Republicans to schedule the session and avert escalation. Bondi, confirmed as AG in February 2025 and ousted by President Trump in early April with Todd Blanche as acting AG, faces compelled testimony despite her departure, though potential legal challenges leave modest uncertainty.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Elon Bull Run Parlay prices "No" at 86%, reflecting the formidable challenge of all three 2026 milestones aligning: Elon Musk reaching $1 trillion net worth per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing another child, and SpaceX executing nine-plus Starship launches to space (≥100 km). As of late April 2026, progress lags critically—Musk's wealth stagnates around $650–800 billion despite Tesla's Q1 earnings beat and SpaceX's February xAI acquisition for orbital AI data centers, with no valuation surge from IPO speculation; zero baby announcements have surfaced; and Starship has logged just one qualifying flight (Flight 12 on April 7) amid V3 testing ramp-up. Key catalysts include May's accelerated launch cadence and potential SpaceX public listing, but the parlay's multiplicative risk keeps Yes a long-shot at 14%.

events.description: More markets for the Süper Lig game, scheduled for May 1 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Beşiktaş JK holds a commanding 4th place in the Süper Lig standings with 55 points from 30 matches, bolstered by scoring 10 goals across their last five games (three wins, including 4-2 over Antalyaspor), while Gaziantep FK languishes 10th on 37 points from 31 outings amid a winless streak in four of five (L-D-L-W-L). Both sides face injury woes—Gaziantep sidelined by long-term absences of wingers Yusuf Kabadayi and Ali Mevran Ablak plus right-back Salem M'Bakata (cruciate tears), and Beşiktaş without key midfielder Kartal Yılmaz (ankle ligaments) and Necip Uysal (muscle). Head-to-head tilts toward Beşiktaş (8-5-4), but Gaziantep's home form at Gaziantep Stadium and recent 2-2 draw offer upset potential amid European qualification stakes.
